mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 9.5 percent, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that compress equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in real estate permits signals a synchronized supply-demand mismatch that erodes corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 150 basis points over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term shows statistical insignificance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of contracting consumer credit on equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises more than 0.25 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when both FEDFUNDS and RSAFS decline simultaneously during tightening phases, or the joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive forward equity returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate profits scale with economic output.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.20 over a 10-year sample, or forward returns are negative during consecutive quarters of positive GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls fall below 130,000 while the unemployment rate simultaneously climbs above 9.0 percent, the labor market loses momentum, triggering a defensive rotation that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS drops below 130000 and UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent
failure_condition: The dual-threshold signal generates a positive predictive value below 40 percent over a 3-year walk-forward test, or SPX forward returns do not significantly diverge from the neutral baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a sustained decline in industrial production fails to ease price pressures, causing real yields to spike and equity multiples to compress, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns turn positive when INDPRO declines during high CPI periods, or the interaction between supply contraction and inflation shows no statistical significance in predicting equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm employment signals robust aggregate demand and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenues to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative information coefficient over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during periods of strong payroll growth.
